idea.topic: What if you fell into a neutron star?
facts: A neutron star's gravity is so strong, it would shrink you to about 1 inch tall due to spaghettification, a process that occurs when tidal forces overcome the internal bonds of an object
The surface temperature of a neutron star can reach up to 500,000 degrees Celsius, which is about 100 times hotter than the surface of the Sun
The density of a neutron star is roughly equivalent to the mass of 1.4 million football fields stacked on top of each other
idea.payoff: and we still don't know what happens to the information contained in matter that falls into a neutron star
idea.title: Neutron Star Fall 🤯
idea.description: Imagine being shrunk to the size of a paper clip and then incinerated by temperatures hotter than the Sun's surface 🌞. The gravity of a neutron star is so extreme, it warps space and time in ways we're still trying to understand 🤯. Could you survive the impossible forces of a neutron star?

scenes.text: WHAT IF YOU FELL INTO A NEUTRON STAR?
scenes.visual: A stylized neutron star silhouette emerges from a dark cosmic background, glowing subtly. Text appears dynamically.

scenes.text: ITS GRAVITY SHRINKS YOU TO 1 INCH! (SPAGHETTIFICATION)
scenes.visual: A close-up of the neutron star, with swirling gravitational distortion effects. An abstract human figure stretches and compresses rapidly into a tiny speck.

scenes.text: SURFACE TEMP: 500,000°C (100x hotter than the Sun!)
scenes.visual: The neutron star's surface pulses with intense, searing heat. A small icon of the Sun appears for comparison, looking dim next to the star's glow.

scenes.text: DENSITY: Mass of 1.4 MILLION football fields
scenes.visual: A graphic depicting a vast field of football fields, rapidly compressing and shrinking into a tiny, incredibly dense sphere representing the neutron star.

scenes.text: BUT WE STILL DON'T KNOW... WHAT HAPPENS TO YOUR INFORMATION.
scenes.visual: The neutron star fades slightly into an enigmatic void, surrounded by abstract, dissolving data streams and question marks, emphasizing the unknown.
